page: Microsoft Azure D64alds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Daldsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs derived from 32 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is supported by a 3780 GB local disk, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. There are no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance.

In benchmark evaluations, the AMD EPYC processor delivers top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for single-core stress-ng CPU tests and PassMark CPU scores. Multi-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% with a stress-ng multi-core score of 118313.81. The processor's instruction set extensions, including AVX-512 and cryptography accelerations like AES and SHA_NI, contribute to top-tier results in PassMark extended instructions and single-threaded gzip compression. Memory performance is also strong, with read-only large block bandwidth reaching 205.53 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 131.87 GB/sec.

The primary architectural tradeoff of the D64alds_v7 is its low memory-to-CPU ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. While this configuration reduces cost relative to memory-optimized instances, it limits suitability for memory-bound workloads. However, the high-capacity 3780 GB local storage and top-tier database operation benchmarks make it highly efficient for compute-heavy database tasks, web serving, and localized data processing. It is also well-suited for lightweight LLM inference, where it achieves top-tier text generation speeds on 7B parameter models.
description: Microsoft Azure D64alds_v7 is an x86_64 server instance powered by a 32-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz, delivering 64 dedicated vCPUs via Microsoft Hyper-V. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, representing a low memory-to-CPU ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, and includes a 3780 GB local disk with Premium Storage capabilities. The instance lacks GPU resources. In benchmarks, the D64alds_v7 demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and PassMark database operations, alongside strong multi-core execution and memory bandwidth. This hardware profile offers a cost-efficient option for compute-intensive workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. It is highly suited for database operations, static web serving, and small-to-medium scale LLM inference tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64alds_v7 is a 64-vCPU server featuring an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, 128 GB RAM, and a 3780 GB local disk, delivering top-tier database and compute performance under Hyper-V.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64alds_v7 is a 64-vCPU server with an AMD EPYC processor, 128 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, optimized for compute-heavy tasks.
tagline: A high-frequency compute instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores, substantial local storage, and a streamlined memory-to-CPU ratio for optimized workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor
128.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per vCPU
3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Optimized for compute-heavy databases, web serving, and LLM inference
